summary: Sign-in page fully restored — fixed a one-character typo in the auth domain that briefly broke the sign-in flow.

items.title: Sign-in flow fully restored
items.description: Right after yesterday's auth-domain split (v3.11.2), the sign-in page stopped loading and showed a blank Kinde page. The cause was a single-character typo in the auth domain configuration: it had been saved as 'auth.thewise.cloud.' (with an extra dot at the end) instead of 'auth.thewise.cloud'. DNS resolution worked the same with or without the trailing dot, which is why the browser still reached Kinde's servers — but Kinde's website routing matches the hostname exactly, so requests with the trailing-dot version fell through to a generic empty page instead of the sign-in form. The configuration was corrected and a fresh deploy was rolled out so the new bundle calls the auth domain with the correct spelling. Sign-in now works end-to-end again.

summary: Sign-in page restored and moved to its own branded address (auth.thewise.cloud) after a Kinde custom-domain misconfiguration briefly made the site unreachable.

items.title: Sign-in flow restored on the live site
items.description: Earlier today, visitors to resume.thewise.cloud were sent straight to the Kinde-hosted sign-in page (with the error "Sorry, we don't see a way to authenticate you at the moment" and no sign-in buttons) instead of the landing page. The cause was a DNS configuration: Kinde's Custom Domain feature was pointed at the same subdomain that already hosts the app, which made the React app unreachable on that hostname. The two roles have been split onto separate subdomains — the app stays on resume.thewise.cloud and the branded sign-in page now lives at the new auth.thewise.cloud — and a fresh deploy was rolled out so the app knows where to send users when they click Sign in. No accounts were affected and no data was lost.

items.title: Branded sign-in URL during authentication
items.description: When you click Sign in or Sign up, the browser will briefly show auth.thewise.cloud in the address bar — that's the same Kinde-themed sign-in card you designed ("Powered by Kinde"), now served from your own subdomain instead of the default thewisecloud.kinde.com. After you sign in, you're redirected back to resume.thewise.cloud as before. This is a cosmetic polish; the sign-in flow itself is unchanged.

summary: Source-control housekeeping: the Replit working copy and the GitHub backup are back in sync after a long drift.

items.title: Replit ↔ GitHub repository reconciled
items.description: The Replit working copy of `main` (which was 161 commits ahead, including the Phase 4 continuous drift-detection scaffolding) and the GitHub `origin/main` (which had Tasks #44–#46 mobile/landing/audit fixes pushed directly) have been merged into a single history. Both sides are preserved as parents of the new merge commit on GitHub — every individual commit from either side is still browseable. Five files conflicted; all resolved in favour of the Replit version, which carried the audit-confirmed truth about which backend functions are deployed. No history was rewritten, no force-push was used. A safety-net branch `sync-from-replit-2026-05-03` was left on GitHub as a parachute and can be deleted from the GitHub UI any time.

items.title: Internal documentation refreshed
items.description: Project Atlas (the internal architecture reference), the governance changelog, and the in-app changelog all updated to record the sync. No user-facing behaviour changed.

summary: Mobile (Capacitor) is buildable end-to-end and the admin desk now shows AI cost attribution per user, feature, and provider.

items.title: AI cost attribution dashboard in the admin desk
items.description: A new panel in the admin Dev Kit breaks down AI credit usage by user, by feature (cover letters, tailoring, interview prep, etc.), and by provider (OpenRouter, Groq, DeepSeek, BYOK). You can finally see *who* is spending and *what* they're spending it on, not just an aggregate total.

items.title: Mobile (Capacitor) build pipeline ready for iOS / Android
items.description: The repo now ships a working mobile build path: `npm run mobile:sync` produces a stripped-down web bundle (no admin tools), syncs it into the native shells, and is ready to open in Xcode (TestFlight) or Android Studio (Play Console). A post-build verifier guarantees no admin code ever leaks into the public mobile binary. The actual binary still has to be produced on a Mac (iOS) or a machine with Android Studio — that's an IDE step, not a CI step.

items.title: Project documentation cleanup
items.description: Internal architecture docs (Project Atlas) updated to accurately describe the new mobile pipeline and to clean up a couple of pre-existing factual inaccuracies (the mobile shell was wrongly described as a PWA wrapper).

summary: DevKit polish: pick a model per AI test slot, and a proper Act As dialog with copyable link and live countdown.

items.title: Per-slot model selection for AI key tests
items.description: Each OpenRouter / Groq / DeepSeek key in DevKit now has its own model dropdown. Pick a different model per slot, save once, and the test button uses it from then on. Solves the OpenRouter free-tier rate-limit that hit when all keys shared the same model, and shows the model that was actually called in the test result line.

items.title: Act As opens a dialog with a copyable link and live countdown
items.description: Clicking 'Act As' on a user no longer auto-pops a tab. You get a small dialog showing the impersonation link in a read-only field with a Copy button, an explicit 'Open in new tab' button, and a live mm:ss countdown driven by the session's expiry — neutral above 2 min, amber under 2 min, red under 30 sec, auto-closes at 0:00 with an 'expired' notice.

summary: Act As now works reliably in any browser tab — no more phantom session-ended toasts.

items.title: Admin Act As session no longer closes immediately
items.description: Fixed three bugs that caused the Act As tab to appear to close right after opening: credentials are now passed securely via the URL fragment (no server round-trip needed, works in both dev and production), the session-end notification uses a reliable cross-tab channel instead of a browser API that misbehaves inside iframes, and a React development-mode quirk that could attempt to start the session twice has been guarded against.

summary: DeepSeek is faster and more resilient, autosave warns when your portfolio is too large to save, and a batch of public-page polish.

items.title: DeepSeek is now faster and more resilient
items.description: We upgraded our DeepSeek connection to a faster model, so AI features that use DeepSeek now reply more quickly. We also added a 60-second safety net so a stuck DeepSeek call no longer holds up your AI request — it quietly retries and, if needed, falls back to another AI provider. And if our DeepSeek account ever runs out of credit, the team now gets a clear alert and can top it up right away, instead of you seeing a vague AI error.

items.title: Autosave now warns once if your portfolio is too large to save
items.description: If your portfolio content (services, case studies, testimonials, translations) grows past the size we can safely save between sessions, you'll see a one-time, friendly warning telling you exactly how much to trim before publishing — instead of silently failing later. Your edits stay in the editor and are still visible in the live preview; only the auto-recovery after a page refresh is paused until you trim back below the limit.

items.title: Polish on your public portfolio page
items.description: A handful of small improvements to the public-page experience: when a browser blocks the print pop-up, you now get an inline link to open the print page in a new tab so you can still save your portfolio as a PDF; the resume-strength tooltip now shows live progress (for example, '1 more skill needed for full strength') as you edit, instead of waiting for a save; and the share button now tells you whether your share went through, you cancelled it, or the link was copied to your clipboard instead, so you're never left guessing what happened.

summary: DeepSeek added as a third AI provider — more resilience, smarter routing

items.title: DeepSeek is now a third AI provider in the engine
items.description: The AI engine now draws from up to 9 keys across three providers — OpenRouter, Groq, and DeepSeek. Keys are picked uniformly at random so load is balanced automatically. When one provider is rate-limited, the engine retries on a sibling key and then spills over to any other available provider. DeepSeek uses the deepseek-chat model and is fully OpenAI-compatible.

items.title: Admins can now pin features to DeepSeek in AI Routing
items.description: The AI Routing panel in the DevKit now lists DeepSeek alongside OpenRouter and Groq as a selectable provider for per-feature routing rules. Admins can force any feature (enhance-section, tailor-resume, etc.) to route through DeepSeek, or use it as a secondary provider in A/B traffic splits.

items.title: DeepSeek tab in DevKit for key inspection and live tests
items.description: A new DeepSeek tab appears in the DevKit alongside OpenRouter and Groq. It shows which of the three key slots are configured (reading DEEPSEEK_KEY for slot 1), displays the last-4-character mask, and lets admins send a live test request to verify each key is working.

summary: Fixed iOS PDF uploads, profile save errors now visible, .mjs MIME type fix

items.title: Fixed iOS PDF upload failure
items.description: Added the missing AddType directive for .mjs files in the Apache server config so the pdfjs-dist web worker is now served with the correct Content-Type on Hostinger. Without this, iOS Safari rejected the module worker script entirely, causing every PDF upload to fail with an empty-text error. Also included .mjs in the long-cache immutable header rule for consistent performance.

items.title: Profile save errors are now visible
items.description: When saving profile information failed (e.g. if the app was still establishing its session on startup, or a network error occurred), the Save sheet would silently do nothing. A clear error toast is now shown so users know to retry.

summary: Fixed 'Profile unavailable' flash on every login

items.title: Fixed post-login 'Profile unavailable' error
items.description: After Kinde authentication, the app was trying to fetch the user profile before the Supabase session bridge finished establishing — causing a 401 error and eventually the 'Profile unavailable' screen. The profile query now waits for the bridge to be ready before firing, so the error no longer appears on login.

summary: DevKit fully operational — all panels restored, missing DB tables created, GitHub connected

items.title: All DevKit panels restored and operational
items.description: 14 edge functions that were missing from the Supabase deployment have been deployed, including Mission Control, Feature Flags, Observability, Integrations, AI Routing, AI Caps, Broadcast, Impersonate, Owner Ops, Resend Stats/Sync, and more.

items.title: Missing database tables created
items.description: Seven new tables are now live in the database: feature_flags, edge_function_logs, kinde_events, portfolio_premium_usernames, broadcasts, ai_routing_config, and email_verification_tokens, along with supporting RPCs and indexes.

items.title: GitHub integration connected in Mission Control
items.description: GitHub credentials (token, owner, repo) are now configured in Supabase secrets. Mission Control will now show live commit history and deployment status instead of the 'GitHub not configured' warning.

items.title: Email verification and password reset flows enabled
items.description: The send-password-reset and verify-email edge functions are now deployed and the supporting email_verification_tokens table has been created.

summary: Fixed manual page-break PDF — content now lands exactly where the break was placed

items.title: Manual page breaks now land exactly where you placed them
items.description: PDF pages generated with the manual breaker now respect the break position precisely. Previously the break would land 33% further into the resume than intended because of a coordinate-space mismatch between the zoom layer and CSS pixel values. Content no longer appears truncated or misaligned on the second page.

summary: Fixed PDF export failing with "Export failed. Please try again."

items.title: PDF export now works in the editor
items.description: The PDF rendering server was not finding its browser binary, causing every export to fail. The server now correctly locates the available browser and generates PDFs as expected.

items.title: Better error message when export is unavailable
items.description: In environments where the PDF rendering server cannot be reached (e.g. certain network configurations), the app now opens your browser's print dialog automatically and guides you to save as PDF, instead of showing a generic "Export failed" error.

summary: Fixed the "View Resume" button after tailoring — it now opens the tailored resume correctly instead of showing a 404 page.

items.title: "View Resume" no longer shows a 404 after tailoring
items.description: After the AI finished tailoring a resume, clicking "View Resume" was navigating to a URL pattern (/editor/{id}) that does not exist in the router, resulting in a 404 page. The tailored resume was created and saved successfully in the database — it just could not be reached. Both the in-editor Tailor sheet and the full Tailor page now navigate to the correct URL (/editor?id={id}), which opens the tailored resume in the editor as expected.

summary: Fixed the Set Target Job analyzer getting stuck on "Generating interview talking points..." forever.

items.title: Set Target Job no longer hangs on the loading screen
items.description: The mobile Set Target Job sheet would appear to load forever after the AI finished analyzing. The result view was crashing while trying to render the list of suggested improvements (the data shape from the server is an object, not a plain string), which left the sheet visually frozen on the progress screen. Both the mobile Set Target Job sheet and the landing page Quick Tailor sheet now display the description text from each improvement, matching the editor view that was already correct. Analyses now complete and show the match score, missing skills, and improvement list as intended.

summary: On mobile, tapping the WiseResume logo at the top of the dashboard now takes you back to the landing page.

items.title: Mobile dashboard logo now returns to the landing page
items.description: Previously, tapping the WiseResume logo at the top-left of the dashboard on mobile only scrolled the dashboard back to the top. It now navigates to the public landing page, matching the standard expectation that a site logo links to the homepage.

summary: Fixed CV uploads (PDF and images) failing on iPhone with the 'text-scanning engine' error. PDF, image, Word, and JSON uploads now all work on iOS Safari and Chrome.

items.title: iPhone CV upload now works for PDF and image files
items.description: Uploading a PDF or photo of a CV on iPhone (Safari or Chrome) was failing with 'Couldn't start the text-scanning engine in this browser (The operation is insecure.)'. The text-scanning engine (used for scanned PDFs and image CVs) was being blocked by the site's security policy because of how it loaded its background worker. The engine now loads its worker directly as a same-origin script, and the security policy was widened to permit worker scripts. iPhone users can now upload any supported CV format the same way desktop users do.

summary: Dashboard Explore section redesigned with a unified compact grid, preview page action buttons no longer hidden behind the nav bar.

items.title: Preview page Edit/Interview/Share buttons now always visible
items.description: The action buttons at the bottom of the preview page were hidden behind the bottom navigation bar on mobile. The spacing now correctly accounts for the full nav bar height including safe-area insets.

items.title: Explore section redesigned as a unified compact grid
items.description: Templates, Examples, Referral, Guides, New Resume, Upload PDF, and Import are now in a single consistent grid — 3 columns on mobile, 4 on desktop — with centered icons and labels, replacing the previous inconsistent two-section layout.

summary: Mobile UX improvements: fixed bottom spacing, scrollable action buttons, and a closeable AI chat button.

items.title: Bottom of the page no longer has an extra black bar on mobile
items.description: A double safe-area calculation was adding unnecessary blank space at the bottom of every page on iPhone. The padding is now accurate, so content fills the screen correctly on all devices.

items.title: Import button is now always reachable on narrow phones
items.description: The three quick-action buttons (New Resume, Upload PDF, Import) were squishing together and cutting off the Import button on smaller screens. They now scroll horizontally so all three are always accessible.

items.title: Wise AI chat button now closes the panel
items.description: Tapping 'Ask' opened the AI chat but there was no visible way to dismiss it from the floating button. The button now toggles — tap it again to close, showing a clear 'Close' label.

items.title: AI Tools and Activity tabs stay put when tapped by free users
items.description: Previously, tapping a Pro-only tab would silently redirect you to the subscription page with no explanation. Now a clear message appears with an upgrade option, and you stay on your current screen.

summary: Editor polish: smarter keyboard handling, cleaner layout, and fully accessible form labels throughout.

items.title: Keyboard shortcuts now work reliably across all editor panels
items.description: Keyboard shortcuts (Undo, Redo, Save, and section navigation) were occasionally ignored when focus moved between editor panels. A new shared keyboard context ensures the correct handler always responds, regardless of which panel is active — no more shortcuts silently doing nothing mid-edit.

items.title: Mobile editor layout simplified
items.description: Removed a redundant mobile layout wrapper that was causing occasional flicker and double-scroll on smaller screens. The editor now uses a single, consistent layout on all screen sizes.

items.title: All form fields in Awards and Projects now have proper labels
items.description: Screen readers and browser auto-fill were skipping several input fields in the Awards and Projects sections because their labels were not correctly linked. Every field now has a properly associated label, making the editor fully accessible and auto-fill-friendly.

items.title: Development logging cleaned up
items.description: Internal debug messages no longer appear in the browser console on the live site. Logging is now restricted to local development builds only.

summary: Fixed PDF upload on iPhone and made OCR work fully offline.

items.title: PDF upload now works on iPhone Safari and Chrome
items.description: Uploading a resume from an iPhone (Create New Resume → Upload PDF) used to fail with a 'Please check your connection' error and the OCR fallback also failed. WiseResume now bundles the PDF reader's font and character-map files alongside the app, so iOS can read embedded-font PDFs the same way desktop browsers do. Desktop and Android upload flows are unchanged.

items.title: OCR (image-based PDFs and scans) works without a third-party CDN
items.description: The OCR engine, its WebAssembly modules, and the English language data are now served directly from resume.thewise.cloud instead of an external CDN. This means OCR works inside the strict security policy of the live site, no longer triggers the connection-error toast, and starts up faster on slow networks.

summary: Maintenance release. No user-facing app changes — internal admin tooling and security hardening only.

items.title: Internal hardening and admin tooling
items.description: Behind-the-scenes improvements to admin tooling and operational safety. Nothing changes in how you use WiseResume — your resumes, dashboard, downloads, and AI features all work exactly as they did in v3.5.9.

summary: New WiseResume favicon — including a proper multi-resolution .ico so the right icon shows up everywhere, including on raw URLs like the changelog.

items.title: Refreshed WiseResume tab icon across the app
items.description: Updated the WiseResume favicon and added a multi-resolution .ico version (16, 32, 48 pixels). Browsers will pick the sharpest one for the tab, the bookmark bar, and the home-screen shortcut. The WiseHire favicon on the enterprises page is unchanged.

summary: Tightened the live-site routing fix from v3.5.7 so secondary endpoints behave the same as the dev server.

items.title: Notifications, jobs, and profile saves stay consistent
items.description: Three follow-ups on the v3.5.7 routing fix: the unread-count call no longer attempts a query against a column name the live database doesn't have; notification mark-read / mark-all-read / delete and the saved-jobs list now return the same simple stub responses the dev server returns; and profile saves return the updated profile in the same shape callers expect.

summary: Restored the dashboard, resumes list, premium plan reflection, and AI status badge on the live site.

items.title: Account, plan, and resumes load again
items.description: Several of the data calls the app makes after sign-in were pointed at a dev-only backend that does not exist on the public site. Hostinger was returning the app shell HTML for those calls instead of JSON, so the dashboard quietly looked empty: no premium reflected, no resumes, no profile. These calls now go straight to the database with your authenticated session, with no shape changes for the rest of the app.

items.title: AI status badge shows real status
items.description: The AI health badge was probing the same dev-only endpoint and always settled into 'unavailable' on the live site. It now pings the live AI service directly, so the badge reflects what AI requests will actually do.

items.title: Clear errors when something is unmapped
items.description: If any future data call slips through without a production route, the app now throws an explicit error instead of silently treating an HTML page as empty data — making this class of regression impossible to miss again.

summary: Restored AI settings, saved keys, and members/resumes data after a long-standing schema gap surfaced once sign-in was working again.

items.title: AI provider settings load again
items.description: Four columns referenced by the AI-keys and account services were missing from the database, so the BYOK panel, the saved-keys list, and parts of the dashboard returned errors as soon as anyone reached them. The columns are now in place with safe defaults; existing saved keys are kept as active.

summary: Fixed the persistent "Sign-in incomplete" error so users can sign in successfully.

items.title: Sign-in completes successfully
items.description: Resolved the underlying cause of the "Your account was verified but we couldn't finish setting up your session" message: a token-validation rule in our auth service was checking the wrong field on Kinde sign-in tokens and rejecting every legitimate sign-in. Sign-in now completes normally on the live site.

summary: Restored automatic deployments and fixed the sign-in session issue caused by a stale live build.

items.title: Sign-in works again on the live site
items.description: The live site had been frozen on an older build for several days because deployments were silently failing, which is why some users saw their email confirmed but couldn't establish a session. The latest build is now live and sign-in completes normally.

items.title: Reliable, fast deployments
items.description: Fixed the file-upload step in the release pipeline so new builds publish in roughly 2-3 minutes again instead of timing out after 13+ minutes.

summary: Every PDF download is now text-selectable, and the editor's score panel is honestly labelled as keyword match.

items.title: Text-selectable PDFs everywhere
items.description: Resumes downloaded from the dashboard's Applications view now produce real text-based PDFs that recruiters and applicant-tracking systems can search and parse, matching what the editor's other download buttons already produced.

items.title: Honest "Keyword Match Score" labelling
items.description: The editor's score panel and related dialogs are renamed to make clear the number measures keyword overlap with your pasted job description, not whether external ATS tools will accept your resume. The score and algorithm are unchanged.

summary: Landing page polish: cleaner section transitions and a calmer scroll experience.

items.title: Cleaner privacy section on the landing page
items.description: Fixed an issue where the last product card could overlap the privacy section as you scrolled past it. The transition between sections is now clean.

items.title: Steadier landing page entrance
items.description: Trust section cards now slide in from the sides without any vertical bounce, and the version label no longer flashes briefly during the first load.

summary: Patch release with stability and reliability improvements across the full 3.5.x cycle.

items.title: Stability and reliability improvements
items.description: This patch consolidates the improvements from the 3.5.x release cycle, ensuring everything works smoothly and consistently for all users.

summary: Pre-production polish: schema sync, API hardening, and faster landing page.

items.title: Snappier landing page
items.description: The home page hero now appears almost immediately on first visit instead of waiting for animations to load.

items.title: Stronger guardrails behind the scenes
items.description: Added automatic checks that prevent broken database references from ever reaching production, and brought the database fully in sync with the app.

items.title: Smoother sign-in and AI features
items.description: Tightened up the AI service connections and a few API endpoints so things stay reliable as the app grows.

summary: This update makes the app clearer and lighter to use, with simpler user messaging, a cleaner version bump, and reduced admin search spam.

items.title: Email search feels faster
items.description: The admin email search now waits for you to pause typing before it looks up users, so the page does less work and feels smoother while you search.

items.title: Plain-language update notes
items.description: The latest changelog entry is written in simple, user-friendly language so you can quickly understand what changed without reading technical details.

items.title: Version updated for this release
items.description: WiseResume has been bumped to the next app version to reflect the newest improvements and keep release tracking accurate.

summary: Ongoing security improvements and reliability fixes to keep WiseResume fast, safe, and dependable.

items.title: We're always working to keep your data safe
items.description: Behind the scenes we've added new layers of protection across WiseResume. Everything is quietly looked after so you can focus on your job search without worrying about the rest.

items.title: Your contact details stay private
items.description: We've made it harder for unwanted bots to collect your contact information from your public portfolio. Real visitors see everything normally — nothing changes for them.

items.title: Ongoing improvements and bug fixes
items.description: This update includes reliability fixes and behind-the-scenes improvements that make WiseResume faster and more dependable for everyone.

summary: Smarter AI error messages, cleaner PDF page breaks, and more reliable sign-in sessions during AI tasks.

items.title: AI errors are now clear and actionable
items.description: When AI isn't configured yet, you'll see a helpful message explaining exactly what's happening — with a direct 'Open Settings' button to fix it in one tap. No more confusing 'temporarily unavailable' messages.

items.title: PDF exports flow cleanly across pages
items.description: Resume sections no longer get cut off awkwardly in the middle when your resume spans more than one page. Each section now starts on the right page and always reads naturally.

items.title: AI keeps working even if your session refreshes
items.description: If your sign-in session expires in the background while you're using an AI feature, WiseResume now recovers automatically and finishes the task — without asking you to sign in again.
